sarga.title: Rama's fight with Trisira -- death of Trisira.

shlokas.original:
खरं तु रामाभिमुखं प्रयान्तं वाहिनीपतिः। 
राक्षसस्त्रिशिरा नाम सन्निपत्येदमब्रवीत्।।3.27.1।।
shlokas.translation: As Khara advanced towards Rama, Trisira the chief of the army approached him and said these words:

shlokas.original:
मां नियोजय विक्रान्त सन्निवर्तस्व साहसात्। 
पश्य रामं महाबाहुं संयुगे विनिपातितम्।।3.27.2।।
shlokas.translation: O valiant one assign this work to me. Refrain from this audacity. See how I am going to put down the mightyarmed Rama in the battle.

shlokas.original:
प्रतिजानामि ते सत्यमायुधं चाहमालभे। 
यथा रामं वधिष्यामि वधार्हं सर्वरक्षसाम्।।3.27.3।।
shlokas.translation: I promise you on my weapon that I will kill Rama.He who killed all demons deserves to be slain.

shlokas.original:
अहं वास्य रणे मृत्युरेष वा समरे मम। 
विनिवृत्य रणोत्साहान्मुहूर्तं प्राश्निको भव।।3.27.4।।
shlokas.translation: Restrain yourself for a moment from the enthusiasm to fight the war. Please act as an arbitrator and see who is killing whom.

shlokas.original:
प्रहृष्टो वा हते रामे जनस्थानं प्रयास्यसि। 
मयि वा निहते रामं संयुगायोपयास्यसि।।3.27.5।।
shlokas.translation: If Rama is killed be happy and go to Janasthana or else if I am killed you can fight.

shlokas.original:
खरस्त्रिशिरसा तेन मृत्युलोभात्प्रसादितः। 
गच्छ युध्येत्यनुज्ञातो राघवाभिमुखो ययौ।।3.27.6।।
shlokas.translation: When Khara was prevailed upon by Trisara in that manner with a wish to face death, Khara  permitted him  by saying 'You may go and fight'.Accordingly he advanced towards Rama.

shlokas.original:
त्रिशिराश्च रथेनैव वाजियुक्तेन भास्वता। 
अभ्यद्रवद्रणे रामं त्रिशृङ्ग इव पर्वतः।।3.27.7।।
shlokas.translation: Trisira who looked a mountain with three peaks also got horses harnessed to a shining chariot and to attack Rama proceeded.

shlokas.original:
शरधारासमूहान्स महामेघ इवोत्सृजन्। 
व्यसृजत्सदृशं नादं जलार्द्रस्य तु दुन्दुभेः।।3.27.8।।
shlokas.translation: Trisira unleashed a stream of arrows like a huge cloud (releasing rain). They produced sound, a drum drenched with water, creates.

shlokas.original:
आगच्छन्तं त्रिशिरसं राक्षसं प्रेक्ष्य राघवः। 
धनुषा प्रतिजग्राह विधुन्वन्सायकान् शितान्।।3.27.9।।
shlokas.translation: On seeing demon Trisira advancing towards him, Rama shook sharp arrows off his bow.

shlokas.original:
स सम्प्रहारस्तुमुलो रामत्रिशिरसोर्महान्। 
बभूवातीव बलिनोस्सिंहकुञ्जरयोरिव।।3.27.10।।
shlokas.translation: The tumultuous combat between Rama and Trisira looked like the strong fight between a lion and an elephant.

shlokas.original:
ततस्त्रिशिरसा बाणैर्ललाटे ताडितस्त्रिभिः। 
अमर्षी कुपितोरामस्संरब्धमिदमब्रवीत्।।3.27.11।।
shlokas.translation: Then Rama hit  with three arrows on the forehead by Trisira flew into a fury and said in a state of great excitement:

shlokas.original:
अहो विक्रमशूरस्य राक्षसस्येदृशं बलम्। 
पुष्पैरिव शरैर्यस्य ललाटेऽस्मिन्परिक्षतः।।3.27.12।।
shlokas.translation: Oh see the strength of this undaunted demon whose arrows fell like flowers on my forehead and only left a scratch.

shlokas.original:
ममापि प्रतिगृह्णीष्व शरांश्चापगुणाच्च्युतान्। 
एवमुक्त्वा तु संरब्धश्शरानाशीविषोपमान्।3.27.13।।
त्रिशिरोवक्षसि क्रुद्धो निजघान चतुर्दश।
shlokas.translation: Receive the arrows released from my bowstring. Saying so, the angry and agitated Rama discharged fourteen arrows, venomous like a snake, on the chest of Trisira .

shlokas.original:
चतुर्भिस्तुरगानस्य शरैः सन्नतपर्वभिः।।3.27.14।।
न्यपातयत तेजस्वी चतुरस्तस्य वाजिनः।
shlokas.translation: With four arrows having strong joints, the mighty hero (Rama) threw down his enemy's four swiftfooted horses.

shlokas.original:
अष्टभिस्सायकैस्सूतं रथोपस्थान्न्यपातयत्।।3.27.15।।
रामश्चिच्छेद बाणेन ध्वजं चास्य समुच्छ्रितम्।
shlokas.translation: Rama brought down the charioteer with eight arrows and with one tore down the lofty banner of the chariot to shreds.

shlokas.original:
ततो हतरथात्तस्मादुत्पतन्तं निशाचरम्।।3.27.16।।
बिभेद रामस्तं बाणैर्हृदये सोऽभवज्जडः।
shlokas.translation: Rama then hit the demon with arrows on his chest while he was running away from the broken chariot. The demon became numb.

shlokas.original:
सायकैश्चाप्रमेयात्मा सामर्षस्तस्य रक्षसः।।3.27.17।।
शिरांस्��पातयद्रामो वेगवद्भिस्त्रिभिश्शितैः।
shlokas.translation: The mighty Rama in fury dismembered the heads of that demon with three sharp and swift arrows.

shlokas.original:
स भूमौ रुधिरोद्गारी रामबाणाभिपीडितः।।3.27.18।।
न्यपतत्पतितैः पूर्वं स्वशिरोभिर्निशाचरः।
shlokas.translation: The demon with his heads decapitated, hit by Rama's arrows a little while ago, fell down, vomitting blood.

shlokas.original:
हतशेषास्ततो भग्ना राक्षसाः खरसंश्रया।।3.27.19।।
द्रवन्ति स्म न तिष्ठन्ति व्याघ्रत्रस्ता मृगा इव।
shlokas.translation: Those surviving demons who were under the protection of Khara got scared and retreated fast and fled like deer frightened by a tiger.

shlokas.original:
तान्खरो द्रवतो दृष्ट्वा निवर्त्य रुषितस्स्वयम्।।3.27.20।।
राममेवाभिदुद्राव राहुश्चन्द्रमसं यथा।
shlokas.translation: Personally persuading the fleeing army to come back, the angry Khara leaped forward towards Rama just as Rahu approaches the moon. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे व��ल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये अरण्यकाण्डे सप्तविंशस्सर्गः।। Thus ends the twentyseventh sarga of Aranyakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
